pending.text: Last Tuesday a plumber in Longview missed a call at 2pm. Guy on the other end called his competitor instead. That job was worth $1,200. The competitor answered on ring two. This happens to you probably twice a week and you don't even notice anymore because you're too busy actually doing the work. That's $4,800 a month walking out the door. Share this with a contractor still losing jobs to voicemail.

Summer's half over. Every week you're not booking jobs is money your friends are already making. We build the whole thing, you work weekends, keep 80%. $0 to start. Send this to whoever keeps saying they're broke but won't answer their phone.

bosssystems.co/summer.html

*Results vary.

Your plumber is probably answering the phone right now while standing in someone's basement covered in sewage.

When that phone rings, he stops what he's doing. Drives back to the office. Books the job. Sends the invoice. That's the whole business.

Except here's the thing. Most plumbers miss the call. They're too busy actually doing the work. So the customer calls the guy who picks up, and now you lost a job worth $800 to $2k.

Your plumber just lost $1,400 to a roofer three blocks away. Same call came in. He answered first. Here's what actually happened. A customer needed work done today. Your guy was busy with paperwork.
